procedure.title: GWPD 1 — Measuring water levels by use of a graduated steel tape

steps.label: Open the well
Chalk the lower few feet of the tape
steps.action_object: lower few feet of the tape

constraints.text: by pulling the tape across a piece of blue carpenter’s chalk

steps.label: Review recent measurements from the well to estimate the hold point
Lower the weight and tape into the well until the lower end of the tape is submerged
steps.action_object: weight and tape into the well

constraints.text: The weight and tape should be lowered into the water slowly to prevent splashing

steps.label: Place thumb and index finger on the tape graduation
steps.action_object: thumb and index finger on the tape graduation

constraints.text: Place the thumb and index finger on the tape graduation that is 0.01 less than the next whole foot mark

steps.label: Continue to lower the tape until thumb and index finger meet the MP
steps.action_object: end of the tape into the well

steps.label: Record the graduation value (the HOLD) in the Hold column of the field form

constraints.text: All water-level data are recorded on the water-level measurement field form (fig. 2) or by using a handheld computer program such as MONKES
Field measurements are recorded to the nearest 0.01 foot or to the appropriate precision based on the judgment of the hydrographer

steps.label: Rapidly bring the tape to the surface

constraints.text: before the wetted chalk mark dries and becomes difficult to read

steps.label: Record the length of the wetted chalk (the CUT) in the Cut row of the field form
Record the time of the measurement in the Time row of the form
Subtract the CUT from the HOLD and record in the WL below MP column
Apply the tape correction to the WL below MP value
steps.action_object: tape correction to the WL below MP value

constraints.text: If the tape-calibration procedure indicates that a correction is needed at a given water-level depth or for a given water-level range, apply that correction to the “WL below MP” value by adding or subtracting the appropriate correction

steps.label: Record the MP correction length on the MP correction row of the field form

constraints.text: the MP correction is positive if the MP is above land surface and is negative if the MP is below land surface (GWPD 3)

steps.label: Subtract the MP correction from the WL below MP value to get depth to water relative to LSD
steps.action_object: MP correction from the WL below MP value

steps.label: Record the water level in the WL below LSD column of the field form
steps.action_object: water level in the WL below LSD column

constraints.text: If the water level is above LSD, record the depth to water in feet below land surface as a negative number

steps.label: Make a check measurement by repeating steps 1 through 5

constraints.text: The check measurement should be made using a different HOLD value than that used for the original measurement

steps.label: Compare the check measurement with the original measurement
steps.action_object: check measurement with the original measurement

constraints.text: If the check measurement does not agree with the original measurement within 0.02 foot, continue to make measurements until the reason for lack of agreement is determined or the results are shown to be reliable

steps.label: Continue measurements until the reason for disagreement is determined or results are reliable
Select the measurement most representative of field conditions
steps.action_object: measurement most representative of field conditions

constraints.text: If more than two measurements are made, use best judgment to select the measurement most representative of field conditions

steps.label: Complete the Final Measurement for GWSI portion of the field form
steps.action_object: Final Measurement for GWSI portion of the field form

steps.label: Disinfect the part of the tape that was submerged
steps.action_object: part of the tape that was submerged

constraints.text: After completing the water-level measurement
as described in the National Field Manual (Wilde, 2004)

steps.label: Rinse the part of the tape that was submerged
steps.action_object: part of the tape that was submerged

steps.label: Close the well
Maintain the tape in good working condition

constraints.text: by periodically checking the tape for rust, breaks, kinks, and possible stretch due to the suspended weight of the tape and the tape weight

steps.label: Recalibrate the tape annually

constraints.text: The tape should be recalibrated annually and recorded in the calibration logbook

steps.label: Record the recalibration in the calibration logbook

constraints.text: All calibration and maintenance data associated with steel tape use are recorded in the calibration and maintenance equipment logbook

steps.label: Read the tape at the top of the oil mark and use this value for the water-level measurement
steps.action_object: tape at the top of the oil mark

constraints.text: If the oil layer is a foot or less thick, read the tape at the top of the oil mark and use this value for the water-level measurement instead of the wetted chalk mark

steps.label: Detect water in the oil using an interface probe or water-detector paste
steps.action_object: presence of water in the oil

constraints.text: if several feet of oil are present in the well, or if it is necessary to know the thickness of the oil layer, an electronic “interface probe,” or a commercially available water-detector paste can be used that will detect the presence of water in the oil
the water level can be estimated by adding the thickness of the oil layer times its density to the oil-water interface altitude

steps.label: Record all calibration and maintenance data in the equipment logbook

REMARKS C324, Date of Field Visit, Equipment ID, TYPE C243) annotated as procedure steps, while Instructions 1-8 (open well, chalk tape, review recent measurements, lower tape and record HOLD, bring tape up and record CUT and time, subtract CUT from HOLD, tape correction, MP correction and WL below LSD) were absent; prior S27 ('Check for oil layer') had no source action. Steps fully re-authored from the Instructions text (29 steps); all 20 constraints grounded to contiguous verbatim source spans (OCR soft hyphens and line-break hyphenation repaired per guidelines Verbatim wording rule; step numbering here supersedes the step IDs referenced in the adjudication log above). Prior C3 folded the purpose sentence 'This will reduce the possibility of contamination...' into the constraint (guidelines: purpose statements are not constraints); replaced by the 'After completing the water-level measurement' precondition (C11) and the NFM reference (C12). 12 -> 20 constraints.
